channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Michelle Yeoh's 1998 National Geographic Photo Shoot Above Hollywood
item.description: In 1998, celebrated actress Michelle Yeoh participated in a daring National Geographic photo shoot captured by renowned photographer Joe McNally. The project, part of a feature story titled 'The Globa...

item.title: Oyo Farmer’s Family Abducted During Coordinated School Attack
item.description: For Oyo State, one serious gbege happen as Micheal Ojo, one local farmer, dey cry over how armed men carry away him four children, him son wife and their small baby from school. The attack occur for Y...

item.title: Arsenal Clinches Premier League Title Under Mikel Arteta After Years of Near-Misses
item.description: Arsenal have finally secured the Premier League title, ending a 984-day wait since their last top-table stint, and affirming the club’s faith in manager Mikel Arteta. Appointed just before Christmas 2...

item.title: Ondo Government Temporarily Closes Schools for APC Assembly Election
item.description: The Ondo State Government don announce say selected public primary and secondary schools go close temporarily ahead of the APC State House of Assembly election wey dey happen on Wednesday, May 20, 202...

item.title: Africa Launches Fellowship to Promote Safe and Inclusive AI Governance
item.description: African countries don start one big initiative wey dey focus on making artificial intelligence (AI) safer, ethical, and more inclusive across the continent. The Policy Innovation Centre, together with...

item.title: Key Economic Shifts Across Africa Amid Inflation and Corporate Dominance
item.description: This week for Africa, plenty things dey happen wey dey shake the economic space. For Nigeria, Aliko Dangote don join Abdul Samad Rabiu and Tony Elumelu as one of di few businessmen wey get multiple co...

item.title: A year on, India-Pakistan tensions remain high with risks of further escalation
item.description: It has been a year since India and Pakistan clashed in a short but intense conflict, triggered by a terrorist attack in Kashmir that killed 26 civilians. The violence escalated quickly, with India lau...

item.title: Madlanga Commission Resumes Proceedings in Pretoria Over Allegations of Corruption and Political Interference
item.description: The Madlanga commission of inquiry has resumed in Pretoria to investigate allegations of criminality, corruption, and political interference within South Africa's criminal justice system. The commissi...

item.title: Calgary to experience unsettled weather on Wednesday
item.description: Calgary residents can expect continued unstable weather conditions on Wednesday, according to Environment Canada. The forecast predicts a mix of showers, gusty winds, and localized thunderstorms throu...

item.title: Philippines urged to pursue energy exploration in West Philippine Sea despite China tensions
item.description: The Philippines has been hesitant to drill for oil and gas in Recto Bank due to concerns over China’s objections. Meanwhile, neighboring countries Malaysia and Indonesia have successfully pursued ener...

item.title: State Senate District 48 Primary Results: Candidates Who Will Challenge Incumbent Shawn Still
item.description: The results for the State Senate District 48 primary have been announced, determining the candidate who will compete against incumbent State Senator Shawn Still in the upcoming November elections. The...

item.title: PPP clarifies no draft seen yet on 28th Constitutional Amendment
item.description: ISLAMABAD – Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP) leaders have stated that it is too early to make any comments regarding the alleged 28th Amendment, which reportedly aims to roll back certain powers devolved ...

item.title: Behind the Scenes: Sophia Loren in the 1972 Film ‘Man of La Mancha’
item.description: The 1972 film adaptation of 'Man of La Mancha' remains a visually striking exploration of idealism, combining a theatrical 'play-within-a-play' approach with a historical setting in a Spanish Inquisit...

item.title: Nigeria Customs launches digital system to speed up airport passenger declarations
item.description: Nigeria Customs Service (NCS) don officially roll out the Simplified Customs Advanced Declaration System (SCADS) for Nnamdi Azikiwe International Airport, Abuja. The system na for make passenger bagga...

item.title: ASA bans misleading claims on DNA self-swab kits for sexual assault victims
item.description: The Advertising Standards Authority (ASA) has banned adverts by Enough, a company providing self-swab DNA testing kits for sexual assault victims, after finding their claims misleading. The adverts, p...

item.title: Photographs Documenting the Assassination of Robert F. Kennedy in 1968
item.description: On June 5, 1968, Senator Robert F. Kennedy, a leading Democratic presidential candidate, was mortally wounded by Sirhan Sirhan at the Ambassador Hotel in Los Angeles, California. Kennedy had just won ...

item.title: Putin-Xi Summit in Beijing Underscores Strong Russia-China Partnership After Trump Visit
item.description: In a significant display of alliance, Russian President Vladimir Putin met Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on Wednesday. The two leaders praised the strong and unyielding relations between the...

item.title: Dis-Chem shareholder controversy sparks debate on Gaza, philanthropy and corporate influence
item.description: Calls to boycott Dis-Chem are growing after majority shareholder Mark Saltzman publicly criticised journalist Redi Tlhabi over Gaza on social media. The dispute began after Tlhabi commented on an arti...

item.title: UCTH reports decline in medical professionals leaving Nigeria
item.description: The Chief Medical Director (CMD) of University of Calabar Teaching Hospital (UCTH), Ikpeme Ikpeme, don yan say the waka of Nigerian medical professionals going abroad don dey slowly reduce. During one...

item.title: Key Developments Across Africa on May 20, 2026
item.description: On 20th May 2026, several major events unfolded across Africa. In Nigeria, joint counterterrorism efforts by Nigerian forces and the U.S. Africa Command resulted in the killing of 175 Islamic State te...
